'''Halowan''' was a barren [[Planet/Legends|planet]] in the [[Fakir sector/Legends|Fakir sector]] of the [[Bakchou arm]]. It was the location of [[Holowan Laboratories/Legends|Holowan Laboratories]], the creators of the IG-series [[Droid/Legends|droids]].

==History==
[[File:IG-88_activated.jpg|thumb|left|250px|The IG-88s massacre their creators.]]
Sometime before [[15 BBY/Legends|15 BBY]], it was the site of the [[Holowan Massacre]], in which the four [[IG-88 assassin droid/Legends|IG-88]] model [[Assassin droid/Legends|assassin droids]] and one [[IG-72]] murdered the entire complement of [[Project Phlutdroid/Legends|Project Phlutdroid]] personnel immediately after activation. They broke free of the lab and blasted away from the planet to begin their [[Droid Revolution]].

Halowan was also home to the [[trans-system data storage library]], an information repository containing information on, among other things, [[Star system/Legends|star systems]] throughout [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. After his service at [[Echo Base/Legends|Echo Base]] ended in [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] historian [[Voren Na'al/Legends|Voren Na'al]] visited the library, compiling a good deal of background information on the [[Hoth system/Legends|Hoth system]] and on the geography of [[Hoth/Legends|Hoth]] for his records.<ref name="GG3">''[[Galaxy Guide 3: The Empire Strikes Back]]''</ref> Shortly after the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]] in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], Na'al was dispatched to Halowan in order to infiltrate the Imperial data storage facilities and attempt to compile a report on the Imperial command structure at [[Endor/Legends|Endor]]. Na'al deceived Imperial security forces into allowing him access to restricted files by posing as a special agent of [[Fakir sector/Legends|Fakir sector]] [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Lorin]]. After one [[Standard day/Legends|day]] of research, Na'al had finished his report and rejoined the [[Alliance Fleet/Legends|Alliance Fleet]].<ref name="GG5">[[Galaxy Guide 5: Return of the Jedi (Second Edition)|''Galaxy Guide 5: Return of the Jedi'', Second Edition]]</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
The PC game [[Star Wars: Rebellion (video game)|''Star Wars: Rebellion'']] mistakenly places the Fakir Sector and, consequently, Halowan, in the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Galactic Core]].

==Appearances==
